. WENATCHEE, Nov. 90 =
. Friends of Charles Deyo, a .
. Great Northern railway brake .


q man, are today talking steps =
. TO bring his name before the =
= Carnegie hero commission for =
. his great exhibition of courage =
= and nerve in preventing =


. catastrophe of the rails Sun .


= day evening. Deyo was rear .
. brakeman on freight train .


= which was pullrRg in on the .


. switch at Dryden, near here, =
= TO allow the Oriental Limited .
. to pass. .
. The freight was behind time =
. and was running at such =
. speed while going onto the .
. siding that Deyo in jumping .
. off TO close the switch was .
= thrown down an embankment .
= and one of his legs broken. .
. Realizing that terrible =
. wreck would be caused by the .
. open switch, Deyo, suffering =
. intense pain and in a half =
= fainting condition, crawled. .
= nearly 10 feet to the switch .
. and closed il just In time to .
= let the speeding limited pass .
= safely by. .
